titlestmt.titleproper: Register to the Adelina Carola Appleton Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Tuesday Rupp

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10584770.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 00:10

did.unittitle: Adelina Carola Appleton Papers
did.abstract: Music and other materials by Adelina Carola Appleton (1886-1958).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open to researchers by appointment. There are no restricted materials in the collection. Please contact the Special Collections staff to schedule an appointment.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Adelina Carola Appleton Papers are the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 128, The Adelina Carola Appleton Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Adelina Carola Appleton (also known as Adeline Carola Appleton or Adelina Carole Appleton) was born in Waverly, Iowa in 1886. She studied music with her mother, at Wisconsin College in Milwaukee, and with Benjamin Blodgett and Carl Eppert. She is best known for her opera about the Salem witch trials, "The Witches' Well" (two of Appleton's own relatives were executed in Salem), for her "Commandos Symphony," which was performed by Arturo Toscanini and the NBC Symphony, and for her symphony "The Phantom." She died in New York in 1958.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists mainly of musical manuscripts by Adelina Carola Appleton. It also includes music by others (including Appleton's mother, Carol Wardwell Lippmann) and miscellaneous other materials.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I. Music. Series II. Miscellaneous.

Music by Adelina Carola Appleton

odd.head: General
odd.p: Photocopies

did.unittitle: [Commandos] Commandos. Schickelgruber's Nightmare, solo piano/by Adelina Carola Appleton.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript with annotations in pencil.

Note on cover: Schickelgruber dreams that he is expiring at the hands of his mortal enemies. Through the mist comes the beat of drums, while the goose-steppers march on. The beat changes into the tap-tap of the coffin-maker, then into bells tolling his knell. Finally the bells ring out with a wild clangor of joy, that he, Schickelgruber, exists no longer.

For voice and piano.

did.unittitle: [Dorothy] Dorothy / music by Adelina Carola Appleton ; words by Richard Middleton

odd.head: General
odd.p: Draft manuscript with annotations in pencil.

did.unittitle: [Dorothy] Dorothy, sketches.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sketches in pencil with annotations in red pencil.

did.unittitle: [Drums of the Sun] Drums of the Sun / Adelina Carola Appleton ; words by Theo Lovett.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Draft Manuscript with sketches on cover, last page.

Note on cover: Great Body Ly-ing a-thwart the sky / with the throbbing sun for a Heart / Let the rhythmic flow of your Light / Beat in my veins - / Give me the Light to see truly / Give me the strength to Live greatly; / Give me the Beat of your Cosmic rhythm / That my Spirit may dance to the Drums of the Sun.

did.unittitle: [Four Lullabies] Four Lullabies. No I. for piano / music by Adelina Carola Appleton.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Draft manuscripts in pencil and ink
Sketch copies included in folder.
No. I: Molto tranquilo.
No. II: The Happy Husband.
No. III: Norse Lullaby.
No. IV: The Doll's Wooing.
Note on first page of No. I: "Last night, while that curfew bell was ringing I heard a moder to her dearie singing Lollyby, lolly, lollyby." -Eugene Fields
Note on first page of No. II: "'Tis when the birds go piping and the daylight slowly breaks." - E.F.

Note on first page of No. III: "The sky is dark and the hills are white / As the Storm King speeds from the North tonight / and this is the song the storm king sings / As over the world his cloak he flings." E.F.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in ink and pencil.

did.unittitle: Music by Others
[With the Wind and the Rain in your Hair] With the Wind and the Rain in your Hair, for piano / music by Clara Edwards
did.physdesc: emph: 31 x 23 cm. (3 p. of music)

did.unittitle: [Three Pieces for Orchestra] Three Pieces for Orchestra, orchestral score / by Ethel Glenn Hier
did.physdesc: emph: 34 x 27 cm. (58 p. of music).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Three movements: Foreboding; Asolo Bells; Badinage.

did.unittitle: [Songs Without Words] Songs Without Words, for piano / by Felix Mendelssohn.
did.physdesc: emph: 34 x 26 cm. (68 p. of music).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Inscription on inside page: To Dear Aunt "Em", in warm love - May many thoughts be brought to your mind of our pleasant hours passed together in the pursuit and love of music, by this little token. Sometime in the future, when we cannot be together, these songs may bring us, at least "in spirit" together. Your Loving Carroll. December 25, '87.

did.unittitle: [You Tell Me I Am Getting Old] You Tell Me I Am Getting Old, for SATB / music by Stanley Krebs, words by Dora Johnson.
did.physdesc: emph: 32 x 24 cm. (1 p. of music).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Photocopy.

did.unittitle: [Nocturne] Nocturne, for solo piano / by A. Borodin.
did.physdesc: emph: 24 x 19 cm. (2 p. of music).

did.unittitle: [I Say Not That Your Beauty] I Say Not That Your Beauty, for voice and piano / music by Joseph McManus; words by Chester A. Arthur [Jr.]
did.physdesc: emph: 32 x 24 cm. (4 p. of music).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in ink and pencil.

did.unittitle: [Song] Song, for voice and piano / music by Kenneth Pitchford; words by James Joyce.
did.physdesc: emph: 35 x 28 cm. (3 p. of music).

odd.head: General
odd.p: On front cover: To Adelina, with Love, Ken.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous
[Collage] Negative photocopy of collage.
did.physdesc: emph: 43 x 56 cm. (5 p. of components).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Negative photocopy of collage of clippings, programs, and other materials. Photocopies of some of the original components are also included.

did.unittitle: [Postcards] Postcards from the National Association for American Composers and Conductors.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Postcard invitations to a performance of "The Witches' Well."
Includes musical quotation by Henry Hadley.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Essay (?) fragment, typescript on notebook paper.

unittitle.emph: [Certificate of Registration of a Claim to Renewal Copyright]

did.physdesc: emph: 8 ½ x 11 in. (2 copies, 4 p.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Photocopies of documents to renew the copyright of "The Witches' Well."
